animal_speaker.name: Animal Speaker
description: ## Animal Speaker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/25›
An animal speaker focuses not on the ears and minds of humans, but on the creatures of the wild and those in the underbellies of cities.

::ab[Animal Friend]{icon=boost-def choice="An animal speaker selects a particular kind of animal, such as apes, badgers, bears, boars, cats, snakes, and so on." order=cp passive="The bard gains a +4 bonus on Handle Animal checks to influence animals of his chosen kind. Animals of this kind have a starting attitude of at least &quot&indifferent&quot& toward the bard and never attack him unless he attacks them first." special="Animal companions and magically controlled animals of the bard's chosen kind can be directed to attack the bard if the controlling creature wins an opposed Charisma check against the bard (this check can be made once per round until it succeeds, after which no further checks are needed). Supernatural versions of animals (such as animals with the ‹template/fiendish› template) can attempt an opposed Charisma check against the bard with a +4 bonus on its roll to overcome this hesitation. This ability has no effect on creatures other than animals." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Nature's Speaker]{icon=boost-magic l5="The animal speaker selects a second kind of animal friend." l11="The animal speaker selects a third kind of animal friend." l17="The animal speaker selects a fourth kind of animal friend." special="The bard can use ‹spell/speak with animals› at will on animals of his selected kinds." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power ability="An animal speaker gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Soothing Performance]{l=3 next icon=power ability="An animal speaker can use bardic performance to influence animals. This works like the druid ability ‹ability/wild empathy›, except he expends 1 round of bardic performance and makes a Perform check. If the bard already has wild empathy from another class, he adds the class levels that provide wild empathy to the result of his Perform check to influence an animal." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Attract Rats]{next l=6 icon=magic ability="The animal speaker can use bardic performance to summon 1d3 ‹monster/rat swarm«s›; they remain as long as he continues performing." imp11="He summons 2d3 swarms instead of 1d3 and the swarms have the ‹template/advanced› creature simple template." imp17="The number of swarms he summons increases to 3d3." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Summon Nature's Ally]{icon=learn ability="At 1st level, the animal speaker adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› to his bard spell list and bard spells known as a 1st-level spell. At 4th level, he adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally II› to his spell list and spells known as a 2nd-level spell, and so on every 3 levels thereafter, until 16th level when he adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally VI› to his 6th-level spell list and spells known." replace="Mass suggestion"}

arcane_duelist.name: Arcane Duelist
description: ## Arcane Duelist
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/80›
A master of the martial applications of steel and spell, the arcane duelist blends both into a lethal combination.
::ab[Arcane Strike]{l=1 icon=power note="Arcane duelists gain ‹feat/Arcane Strike› as a bonus feat at 1st level." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power ability="An arcane duelist gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Rallying Cry (Su)]{next icon=boost l=1 flavor="An arcane duelist can use performance to rally dispirited allies." ability="Each round he makes an Intimidate check. Any ally (including the bard) within 30 feet may use this check in place of his own saving throw against fear and despair effects. Those already under a fear or despair effect can attempt a new save each round using the bard's Intimidate check." special="Rallying cry does not work on effects that don't allow saves. This is a mind-affecting ability that uses audible components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Bladethirst (Su)]{next l=6 icon=boost ability="An arcane duelist may use performance to grant one weapon, one natural weapon, one end of a double weapon, or 50 items of ammunition of the same type within 30 feet a +1 enhancement bonus. This enhancement bonus increases by +1 for every three levels after 6th (maximum +5 at 18th level)." special="These bonuses stack with existing bonuses and may be used to increase the item's enhancement bonus up to +5 or to add any of the following weapon properties: ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/ghost touch›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, ‹magic-enh/mighty cleaving›, ‹magic-enh/returning›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, ‹magic-enh/seeking›, ‹magic-enh/speed›, or ‹magic-enh/wounding›. If the weapon is not magical, at least a +1 enhancement bonus must be added before adding special abilities." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Mass Bladethirst (Su)]{next l=18 icon=boost ability="An arcane duelist can use his *bladethirst* performance to enhance the weapons of as many allies as desired within 30 feet. The bonus provided by this power is +4 if conferred on two allies, +3 for three allies, +2 for four allies, and +1 for five or more allies. The power granted to each weapon must be identical." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power flavor="An arcane duelist weaves might and magic in his combat style." l2="Gain ‹feat/Combat Casting› as a bonus feat" l6="Gain ‹feat/Disruptive› as a bonus feat" l10="Gain ‹feat/Spellbreaker› as a bonus feat" l14="Gain ‹feat/Penetrating Strike› as a bonus feat" l18="Gain ‹feat/Greater Penetrating Strike› as a bonus feat" replace="Versatile performance~well-versed"}

::ab[Arcane Bond (Ex)]{icon=power l=5 ability="An arcane duelist gains the arcane bond ability as a ‹class/wizard›, using a weapon as his bonded item, allowing him to cast any one addition spell that he knows once per day." special="He may not choose a familiar or other type of bonded item. He may use the hand holding his bonded weapon for somatic components." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Arcane Armor (Ex)]{icon=def-power l10="An arcane duelist gains Medium Armor Proficiency and can cast bard spells in medium armor with no chance of arcane spell failure." l16="He gains Heavy Armor Proficiency and can cast bard spells in heavy armor with no arcane spell failure chance." replace="Jack of all trades"}

arcane_healer.name: Arcane Healer
description: ## Arcane Healer
‹SOURCE Faiths and Philosophies/13›

Though bards may have different reasons for wanting to heal the sick and injured, both out of pure altruism and in order to keep adventuring companions safe, the ability to heal also makes it much easier for a bard to pose as a cleric or other divine healer, opening the door for a wide array of swindles and scams.

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{l=2 usage="Once a day" icon=power-aura standard="An arcane healer gains the ability to ‹ability/channel positive energy› as a cleric of one level lower than his level. If a creature within the channeled energy's area of effect would be allowed a save against its effects, the DC is equal to 10 + ‹misc/half› the arcane healer's level + the arcane healer's Charisma modifier." imp6="He can *channel* twice a day." imp10="He can *channel* three times a day." imp14="He can *channel* four times a day." imp18="He can *channel* five times a day." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Inspiring Healing (Sp)]{icon=magic flavor="An arcane healer gains the ability to expend rounds of his bardic performance to provide extra healing to his allies." l5="By expending 2 rounds of bardic performance, an arcane healer can cast ‹spell/cure light wounds› as a spell-like ability." l11="The arcane healer can expend 2 rounds of bardic performance to cast ‹spell/cure moderate wounds› as a spell-like ability" l17="He may instead expend 2 rounds of bardic performance to cast ‹spell/cure serious wounds› as a spell-like ability." special="No single target may be affected by an arcane healer's inspiring healing ability more than once in a 24-hour period." replace=Loremaster}

archaeologist.name: Archaeologist
description: ## Archaeologist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/32›

No stodgy researcher, this archaeologist meets his research head-on in the field. Archaeologists sacrifice the bard's inspirational performance for a smattering of rogue talents. This archetype also fits rogue-like characters that focus more on learning than on stabbing foes in the back.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{icon=down note="Archaeologists do not gain the *bardic performance* ability or any of its performance types."}

::ab[Archaeologist's Luck (Ex)]{icon=boost-def flavor="Fortune favors the archaeologist." swift="An archaeologist can call on fortune's favor, giving him a +1 luck bonus on attack rolls, saving throws, skill checks, and weapon damage rolls. Maintaining this bonus is a @HLfree_action, but it ends immediately if the archaeologist is killed, paralyzed, stunned, knocked unconscious, or otherwise prevented from taking a free action to maintain it each round." useMod=Cha4 useUnit=round special="Archaeologist's luck is treated as bardic performance for the purposes of feats, abilities, effects, and the like that affect bardic performance. Like bardic performance, it cannot be maintained at the same time as other performance abilities." increment="The bonus~-1~6"}

::ab[Clever Explorer (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="An archaeologist gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his class level on Disable Device and Perception checks. He can disable intricate and complex devices in half the normal amount of time (minimum 1 round) and open a lock as a @HLstandard_action." imp6="An archaeologist can take 10 on Disable Device checks, even if distracted or endangered, and can disarm magical traps." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{icon=def l=2 ability="An archaeologist gains ‹ability/uncanny dodge›, as the rogue class feature of the same name." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Trap Sense (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def ability="An archaeologist gains ‹ability/trap sense›, as the rogue class feature of the same name. The bonus granted by *trap sense* is equal to one-third of your archaeologist level."}

::ab[Rogue Talents]{l=4 icon=power ability="An archaeologist gains a ‹ability/rogue talent›. He gains an additional rogue talent for every four levels of archaeologist gained after 4th level. Otherwise, this works as the rogue's rogue talent ability."}
::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{l=6 icon=def ability="An archaeologist gains ‹ability/evasion›, as the rogue ability of the same name."}
::ab[Advanced Talent]{l=12 icon=boost ability="An archaeologist can now choose an ‹ability/advanced rogue talent› instead of a rogue talent."}

archivist.name: Archivist
description: ## Archivist
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/80›
Some bards greatly prefer academic pursuits to the drama (and sometimes melodrama) of their artistic brethren.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{icon=power head note="An archivist gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Naturalist (Ex)]{next icon=boost ability="An archivist who has identified a creature with a Knowledge check appropriate to its type can use performance to share strategies for defeating it with allies in combat. The archivist and any allies within 30 feet gain a +1 insight bonus to AC and on attack rolls and saving throws against exceptional, supernatural, and spell-like abilities used by creatures of that specific kind of monster (e.g., ‹monster/frost giant«s›, not all giants or all humanoids)." increment="The bonus~-1~6" special="This language-dependent ability requires visual and audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Lamentable Belaborment (Ex)]{next l=6 icon=lower ability="An archivist can bewilder a creature already ‹misc/fascinated› by his performance. Using this ability does not disrupt the fascinate effect, but it does require a @HLstandard_action to activate (in addition to the @HLfree_action to continue the fascinate effect). The target must make a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier). Success renders the target immune to this power for 24 hours, but failure leaves the target either ‹misc/dazed› or ‹misc/confused› (archivist's choice) for as long as the performance continues." special="If the target takes damage, this effect ends immediately. This mind-affecting ability relies on audible components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Pedantic Lecture (Su)]{next l=18 icon=boost ability="An archivist can affect as many creatures with *lamentable belaborment* as he currently has fascinated. In addition, he may choose to cause targets to fall asleep rather than be dazed or confused." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Lore Master (Ex)]{icon=boost l2="An archivist may take 20 on Knowledge checks once per day." l8="He may do so twice a day." l14="He may do so three times a day." l20="He may do so four times a day." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Magic Lore (Ex)]{icon=boost-def passive="An archivist gains a bonus on Spellcraft checks to identify magic items or decipher scrolls equal to half his bard level and may take 10 on such checks. He gains a +4 bonus on saves against magical traps, language-dependent effects, and symbols, glyphs, and magical writings of any kind." ability="An archivist can use Disable Device to disarm magical traps as per a rogue's ‹ability/trapfinding› ability." order=pa replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Jack-of-All-Trades (Ex)]{icon=power l5="An archivist can use any skill, even if the skill normally requires him to be trained." l11="He considers all skills to be class skills." l17="He can take 10 on any skill check, even if it is not normally allowed." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Probable Path (Ex)]{icon=roll flavor="An archivist can calculate the action likely to bring success with the least risk" l10="Once per day, he can take 10 on any d20 roll." l13="He may do so twice a day." l16="He may do so three times a day." l19="He may do so four times a day." replace="Jack of all trades"}

argent_voice.name: Argent Voice
description: ## Argent Voice
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/174›

As much as the ‹rule/Silver Ravens› are freedom fighters, they are also patrons of the arts. Perhaps owing to the strong ties between the organization's leadership and the Kintargo Opera House, the Silver Ravens are particularly active in the theater community and count many of Cheliax's finest singers among their recruits. In part, this is mere shrewdness, as actors and performers are often invited into the halls of the wealthy and powerful, where a rebellion can do the most damage.

However, there is another reason for the Silver Ravens to recruit singers from the Chelish opera. The Silver Ravens rely on talented voices to deploy their most powerful weapon in the defense of Kintargo - the "Song of Silver." While specially trained argent dramaturges can create lesser versions of this song, the true "Song of Silver" must be performed in Kintargo. Those among the Silver Ravens who dedicate themselves to the study of this magical performance are known as argent voices, and through practice and experience, these bards are able to modulate their own bardic performance abilities to reflect some measure of the power found in the "Song of Silver." For this reason, argent voices are among the most respected and treasured of the Silver Raven's agents. While some argent voices travel the Inner Sea, fighting tyranny and oppression, many others remain in Kintargo, ready to activate the city's defenses should the need arise.
Argent voices who wish to further explore the magic and power of the "Song of Silver" often further focus their training so that they can become ‹pclass/argent dramaturge«s›.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="An argent voice gains the following bardic performances. The argent voice must use Perform (sing) with these performances."}

::ab[Limning Verse (Su)]{next icon=lower-aura ability="An argent voice can sing a song that causes fiends to glow with an aura of silvery light. Outsiders with the evil subtype within 10 feet of the argent voice are affected as per ‹spell/faerie fire›." imp4="The range of this ability increases by 10 feet for every four argent voice levels he has, to a maximum of 60 feet at 20th level." special="Any defense that prevents the detection of alignment or evil also prevents *limning verse* from outlining a creature." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Shattering Crescendo (Sp)]{next flavor="The argent voice learns to sing at frequencies capable of shattering magical effects." l=6 icon=magic fullround="An argent voice can use 2 rounds of bardic performance to attempt to dispel a single evil spell or enchantment spell cast by an evil creature. This functions as per ‹spell/dispel magic›, using the bard's level as his caster level." imp18="This ability can be used on effects that can be removed by the spell ‹spell/break enchantment›." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion"}

::ab[Devilbane Refrain (Su)]{next l=8 icon=boost flavor="The argent voice learns how to mimic aspects of the magical &quot&Song of Silver&quot& with his performance, enhancing his allies' ability to combat devils and similar fiends." ability="A creature must be able to hear the argent voice's singing and be within 30 feet of him to be affected. Natural and manufactured weapons wielded by affected allies are treated as though they were silver for the purpose of bypassing damage reduction." imp14="Allies' natural and manufactured weapons also gain the benefit of the evil outsider ‹magic-enh/bane› weapon special ability." replace="Dirge of doom~frightening tune"}

::ab[Dedicated Performance (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="An argent voice can call upon the training of the Chelish opera to influence and scrutinize his foes." l2="An argent voice must choose Perform (sing) as his versatile performance skill." l6="The argent voice gains a bonus on Perform (sing) checks made as part of a versatile performance equal to the number of **additional** Perform skills in which he has the maximum number of ranks possible at his level." l10="The bonus increases to +2 per additional maximized Perform skill." l14="The bonus increases to +3 per additional maximized Perform skill." l18="The bonus increases to +4 per additional maximized Perform skill." alter="Versatile performance"}

arrowsong_minstrel.name: Arrowsong Minstrel
description: ## Arrowsong Minstrel
‹SOURCE Arcane Anthology/30›
Originating from the wooded wilds of Hymbria, Arrowsong minstrels combine the elven traditions of archery, song, and spellcasting into a seamless harmony of dazzling magical effects.
::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=ranged-down note="An Arrowsong minstrel is proficient with ‹eq-weapon/longbow«s›, but not the longsword, rapier, or whip." alter="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down-magic note="An Arrowsong minstrel casts one fewer spell of each level than normal. If this reduces the number to 0, she can cast spells of that level only if her Charisma score allows ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Arcane Archery]{icon=learn-boost flavor="An Arrowsong minstrel's skill at ranged martial arts allows her to learn to cast a number of powerful, offensive spells that would otherwise be unavailable to her." l1="She selects a number of spells from the sorcerer/wizard spell list (see *special* below) equal to her Charisma modifier (minimum 1) and adds them to her bard spell list as bard spells of the same level. The spells selected must not already be on the bard spell list, and must be 6th level or lower." l4="She adds an additional sorcerer/wizard spell to her bard spell list." l8="She adds an additional spell to her bard spell list." l12="She adds an additional spell to her bard spell list." l16="She adds an additional spell to her bard spell list." l20="She adds an additional spell to her bard spell list." special="An Arrowsong minstrel is limited to the following spells when adding spells to her spell list in this manner: ‹spell/acid arrow›, ‹spell/arrow eruption›, ‹spell/flame arrow›, ‹spell/gravity bow›, ‹spell/greater magic weapon›, ‹spell/longshot›, ‹spell/magic weapon›, ‹spell/protection from arrows›, ‹spell/spectral hand›, ‹spell/true strike›, and sorcerer/wizard spells of the ‹main/spells_>evocation› school. An Arrowsong minstrel must still select these spells as spells known before she can cast them." passive="For the purpose of meeting the requirements of combat feats and prestige classes, an Arrowsong minstrel treats her bard level as her base attack bonus (in addition to base attack bonuses gained from other classes and Hit Dice). Her skilled blending of archery and arcane magic also allows an Arrowsong minstrel to fulfill a spell's somatic components even when using a bow in both hands (though not other ranged weapons)." replace="Bardic knowledge~dirge of doom~distraction~fascinate~inspire competence~lore master~soothing performance"}

::ab[Precise Minstrel (Ex)]{icon=power-lower l=2 order=ap ability="An Arrowsong minstrel gains ‹feat/Precise Shot› as a bonus feat." passive="In addition, any creature that is affected by any of the Arrowsong minstrel's bardic performance does not provide soft ‹rule/cover› to enemies against her ranged attacks with a bow." replace="The versatile performance gained at 2nd level~well-versed"}

::ab[Arrowsong Strike (Su)]{icon=magic-ranged l6="An Arrowsong minstrel can use spellstrike (as per the ‹class/magus› class feature) to cast a single-target ranged touch attack spell and deliver it through a ranged weapon attack. Even if the spell can normally affect multiple targets, only a single missile, ray, or effect accompanies the attack. She cannot use spellstrike with normal touch attacks or melee attacks." l18="An Arrowsong minstrel using a multiple-target spell with this ability can deliver one ray or line of effect with each attack when using a ‹rule/full-attack› action, up to the maximum allowed by the spell (in the case of ray effects). Any effects not used in the round the spell is cast are lost." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion"}

averaka_arbiter.name: Averaka Arbiter
description: ## Averaka Arbiter
‹SOURCE Bastards of Golarion/15›

Some Averakan bards make it their mission to unify their fellow half-orcs, and are equally skilled at helping allies defeat common enemies and at building a new civilization that reflects their unique identity.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="An Averaka arbiter gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Inspire Teamwork (Ex)]{l=3 next icon=boost ability="An Averaka arbiter can use his performance to treat his allies as if they possessed the same ‹main/teamwork feat«s› as the bard for the purpose of determining whether the bard receives a bonus from his teamwork feats. His allies do not receive any bonuses from these feats unless they actually possess the feats themselves. The allies' positioning and actions must still meet the prerequisites listed in the teamwork feat for the bard to receive the listed bonus." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Ritual of Reconciliation (Su)]{l=8 next icon=boost ability="An Averaka arbiter can use his performance to ‹skill/improve the attitude«s› of nearby creatures by two steps. To be affected, a creature must be within 30 feet and able to hear the bard. Creatures whose attitude toward the bard becomes indifferent or better immediately cease attacking the bard and his allies, but may otherwise act as they please. The effect persists for as long as the creature is within 30 feet and the bard continues his performance. The effect ends if one of the bard's allies attacks the creature. A successful Will saving throw (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) negates the effect." special="*Ritual of reconciliation* relies on audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}
::ab[Versatile Teamwork (Ex)]{icon=power l2="An Averaka arbiter gains a bonus teamwork feat." repeat="He gains an additional bonus teamwork feat.~6~4" replace="Versatile performance~well-versed"}

brazen_deceiver.name: Brazen Deceiver
description: ## Brazen Deceiver
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/50›

Where other thieves use stealth or intimidation to achieve their goals, the brazen deceiver depends on lies. The ‹rule/Council of Thieves› often sends brazen deceivers to acquire blackmail materials, arrange the release of incarcerated agents, or otherwise manipulate events in the Council's favor. Brazen deceivers' techniques come from a dark source. During the reformation of the Council, Sabriune and Aspexia acquired the shattered fragments of an ancient relic called the Totemrix, which belonged to the long-deceased demon lord of shadows, Vyriavaxus. This particularly intrigued Sabriune, for it was her demonic patron Nocticula who slew Vyriavaxus. Drawing upon Sabriune's heretical faith, Aspexia's psychometric mastery, and Westcrown's lingering psychic trauma from an oppressive shadow curse that plagued its nights for many years, the Council leaders were able to coax remnants of power from the broken artifact. All brazen deceivers are imbued with a fragment of that power during their initiation - not enough to damn a soul or shift alignment, but just enough to give these bards access to the sinister energies they need to deceive.

::ab[Deceptive Tale (Su)]{icon=power icon=boost ability="A brazen deceiver learns the *deceptive tale* bardic performance, allowing him to weave magic into his lies and imbue the most fantastic claims with the appearance of truth. While the brazen deceiver maintains this performance, he takes half the normal penalty on Bluff checks for unlikely lies (rounding down to -2)." imp5="This effect also applies to Bluff checks for far-fetched lies." imp11="It now applies to Bluff checks for impossible lies." special="*Deceptive tale* relies on audible components." replace=Countersong~distraction}
::ab[Shameless Scoundrel (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A brazen deceiver adds ‹misc/half› his level (minimum +1) on Bluff, Disguise, and Stealth checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Blatant Subtlety (Ex)]{icon=def ability="A brazen deceiver has mastered the art of using magic without being detected. The brazen deceiver gains ‹feat/Spellsong› as a bonus feat. Observers do not automatically recognize his bardic performances as anything other than ordinary speech or performance. Those specifically looking for abnormal effects must succeed at a Sense Motive check (DC = 10 + half the brazen deceiver's bard level + the brazen deceiver's Charisma modifier) to detect his performances." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Invoke Vyriavaxus (Ex)]{icon=learn note="Westcrown suffered under the shadow curse for many years, and a brazen deceiver has a tiny piece of that darkness lodged in his soul; as his skills increase, he learns to command this shadowy power. A brazen deceiver adds the following spells to his bard spells known at the listed class levels." imp2="The brazen deceiver adds ‹spell/bleed› and ‹spell/touch of fatigue› to his 0-level bard spells known." imp6="The brazen deceiver adds ‹spell/darkness› and ‹spell/darkvision› to his 2nd-level bard spells known." imp10="He adds ‹spell/shadow conjuration› and ‹spell/shadow step› to his 4th-level bard spells known." imp14="He adds ‹spell/shadow evocation› and ‹spell/shadow walk› to his 5th-level bard spells known." imp18="He adds ‹spell/shadow conjuration, greater› and ‹spell/greater shadow evocation› to his 6th-level bard spells known." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Devil's Tongue (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A brazen deceiver beguiles others with astonishing skill. This functions as the *lore master* ability, but its effects apply to Bluff checks instead of Knowledge skill checks." replace="Lore master"}

buccaneer.name: Buccaneer
description: ## Buccaneer
‹SOURCE Pirates of the Inner Sea/22›

A buccaneer is a pirate who specializes in raiding ships and port towns, but who leaves his victims alive whenever possible. Buccaneers are flashy, debonair pirates who enjoy the excitement and rewards of piracy, but prefer not to kill; they are welcome on ships whose captains prefer to avoid the punishments that murder would bring or who lack the heart to strike down civilians in cold blood. Buccaneers are also popular in privateer crews - and among slave traders.

::ab[Hilt Bash (Ex)]{icon=power flavor="A buccaneer develops the ability to fight with the hilts and flats of his weapons, aiming to leave his victims alive." ability="He can use a weapon that normally deals lethal damage to deal nonlethal damage with no penalty on his attack roll." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Song of Surrender (Su)]{l=4 icon=lower ability="A buccaneer can use his performance to encourage an enemy to surrender. To be affected, an enemy must be within 30 feet and be able to see and hear the buccaneer's performance. An affected enemy feels the irresistible urge to drop any held weapons and fall ‹rule/prone›. This effect lasts for 1 round - essentially, the affected enemy takes no actions on its next turn other than to lie prone, although it is not considered ‹rule/flat-footed› or ‹misc/helpless›." special="A Will saving throw (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the buccaneer's level + the buccaneer's Charisma modifier) negates the effect. This ability affects only a single creature. Song of surrender is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting, language-dependent ability and relies on audible components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Knock Out (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power flavor="A buccaneer may focus his blows in an attempt to knock out an opponent." swift="The buccaneer can choose one target to attempt to knock out. The buccaneer adds his Charisma bonus (if any) on his attack roll and adds his buccaneer level on any nonlethal damage rolls made against the target. The bonus lasts until the buccaneer deals nonlethal damage to his target or until the buccaneer chooses a new target to attempt to knock out." useInc="6~bard~5" useF="5~1~6" replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Mass Song of Surrender (Su)]{l=16 icon=boost ability="This ability functions just like *song of surrender* but allows a buccaneer to affect all enemies within 30 feet. Enemies within range of this effect still receive a saving throw." special="*Mass song of surrender* is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting, language-dependent ability that relies on audible components." replace="Mass suggestion"}

busker.name: Busker
description: ## Busker
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/26›

A busker uses his dazzling physical stunts to get by on his own in the city streets. Buskers commonly use their skills to entertain for a few coins, but also pick pockets, hide in plain sight as statues, and manipulate the desperate in medicine shows. Many buskers are also ‹arc-bard/juggler«s›.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{icon=down note="Buskers do not gain the bardic performance ability or any of its performance types, except as noted below."}

::ab[Busker Stunts (Su)]{head icon=boost useMod=Cha4 useUnit=round ability="A busker knows how to magically enhance the effectiveness of his physical stunts. Maintaining a stunt is a @HLfree_action, but it ends immediately if the busker is killed, paralyzed, stunned, knocked unconscious, or otherwise prevented from taking a free action to maintain it each round." special="This ability is treated as bardic performance for the purposes of feats, abilities, and other effects that affect bardic performance. Like a bardic performance, a busker stunt cannot be maintained at the same time as other performance abilities."}

::ab[Quick Hands (Su)]{next l=1 icon=boost-def swift="A busker can call on his stunts to gain a +1 competence bonus on Acrobatics checks, a +1 dodge bonus to AC and on Reflex saves, and a +1 bonus on attack rolls as if from ‹spell/haste›." increment="This bonus~-1~6" imp5="While maintaining this stunt the busker also gains an extra attack at his highest base attack bonus as if from *haste.*"}

::ab[Patter (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A busker can engage in brisk conversation that seems innocuous, but actually encourages creatures to reveal secrets to the busker. Each round while maintaining this stunt, the busker can force one creature within 30 feet that is able to see and hear the busker and has an attitude of indifferent or better towards him to attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the busker's bard level + the busker's Charisma modifier). On a failed save, the target must answer one yes/no question the busker asks to the best of its ability." special="Anyone observing this (including the creature asked, even if it succeeds at its save) must succeed at a Sense Motive check (opposed by the busker's Bluff check) to realize the question was asked or answered. A target that feels threatened or is in combat is immune to this ability. This is a language-dependent, mind-affecting enchantment effect."}

::ab[Living Statue (Su)]{next l=3 icon=def ability="A busker can use this stunt to hold perfectly still. While maintaining this stunt the busker can attempt a Bluff check (opposed by all observers' Sense Motive checks) to appear paralyzed, dead, or as a statue (this requires that the busker be disguised to look as though he were made of stone or metal, which is aided with a ‹eq-misc/statue disguise kit›). Even magical means of determining the true nature of the busker do not function for an observer that fails this check. If the busker takes any physical actions, this stunt ends."}
::ab[Fascinate (Su)]{next l=6 icon=power ability="The busker can ‹misc/fascinate› creatures with his stunts, as per the bardic performance ability."}
::ab[Suggestion (Sp)]{next l=8 icon=power ability="The busker can implant a ‹spell/suggestion› in a creature with his stunts, as per the bardic performance ability of the same name."}

::ab[Inventive Juggler (Ex)]{next l=9 icon=melee flavor="A busker masters unconventionally dangerous juggling techniques." ability="While maintaining this stunt, the busker can use a thrown weapon (even an improvised one) to threaten spaces as though he were using a ‹misc/reach› weapon." imp12="The busker threatens squares up to 10 feet farther away with thrown weapons." imp15="The busker now threatens squares up to 20 feet farther away." imp18="The busker now threatens squares up to 30 feet farther away."}

::ab[Impossible Sleight of Hand (Su)]{next l=15 icon=magic ability="A busker can store items in or retrieve them from a personal extradimensional space with a maximum capacity of 8,000 cubic feet and 8,000 pounds as a busker stunt. The item may be sent from or called to his hand or any place it fits on his person. He can access the extradimensional space only to store or retrieve a single object at a time; to retrieve or store an object larger than Fine size, the busker must expend 1 additional round of busker stunts for each size category larger than Fine." special="This stunt is instantaneous and must be activated again each time the busker wishes to use it. The extradimensional space empties its contents within the busker's space if he dies."}

::ab[Flexible Performer (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A busker can attempt Acrobatics, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth checks to earn money instead of Perform checks. He can use his Bluff modifier in place of his Diplomacy modifier on Diplomacy checks."}

celebrity.name: Celebrity
description: ## Celebrity
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/25›

Known for being known, a celebrity bard is a master of performance who captures the imagination and attention of his audience. He trades on his charisma, his wit, and his exploits to build his renown - and that of his companions.

::ab[Famous]{l=1 icon=boost choice="A celebrity bard may choose a region where he is famous, and within that region, the locals are more likely to react favorably toward the bard. The bard gains a bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks in that area and to influence people from that area." imp1="This region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of 1,000 or fewer people, and the modifier on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks is +1. As the celebrity grows more famous, additional areas learn of him (typically places where he has lived or traveled, or settlements adjacent to those where he is known) and his bonuses apply to even more people." imp5="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of 5,000 or fewer people, and the modifier on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks is +2." imp9="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of up to 25,000 people, and the modifier on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks is +3." imp13="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of up to 100,000 people, and the modifier to Diplomacy and Intimidate is +4." imp17="The bard's renown has spread far, and most civilized folk know of him (GM's discretion); the bard's modifier on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks is +5." replace="Inspire courage"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A celebrity gains the following type of bardic performance."}

::ab[Gather Crowd (Ex)]{next l=5 icon=power ability="The celebrity is skilled at drawing an audience to his performances. If he is in a settlement or populated area, he can shout, sing, or otherwise make himself noticed in order to attract an audience to his impromptu stage. The size of the crowd depends on the local population, but typically is a number of people equal to ‹misc/half› the bard's class level &times& the result of the bard's Perform check. The crowd gathers over the next 1d10 rounds." special="If the bard fails to engage the crowd (such as by performing, kissing babies, trying to use fascinate, and so on), it disperses over the next 1d10 rounds." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Shining Star (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost passive="The celebrity has learned how to focus attention on himself so thoroughly that even the presence of danger does not distract his adoring crowd. When using *fascinate,* a target making a save to break the effect because of a potential threat takes a -4 penalty on that save, and even obvious threats require a save rather than automatically breaking the effect. Creatures affected by the bard's fascinate ability ignore the shaken condition." replace="Dirge of doom"}

chelish_diva.name: Chelish Diva
description: ## Chelish Diva
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Magic/32›

Cheliax is well known for its elaborate operas, and as with any kind of theater, there are performers whose egos get the better of them. Some have an inflated idea of their talent and importance. A few, the Chelish divas, are wholly justified in their feelings of superiority. In their pursuit of glory, their magic and discipline allows them to prove themselves better than any upstart actor, dancer, or chorus member - and when crossed, their fury is legendary. A Chelish diva has the following class features.

::ab[Famous]{l=1 icon=boost choice="A Chelish diva may choose a region where she is famous, and within that region, the locals are more likely to react favorably toward her. The bard gains a bonus on Bluff and Intimidate checks in that area and to influence people from that area." imp1="This region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of 1,000 or fewer people, and the modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +1. As the diva grows more famous, additional areas learn of her (typically places where she has lived or traveled, or settlements adjacent to those where she is known) and her bonuses apply to even more people." imp5="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of 5,000 or fewer people, and the modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +2." imp9="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of up to 25,000 people, and the modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +3." imp13="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of up to 100,000 people, and the modifier to Bluff and Intimidate is +4." imp17="The diva's renown has spread far, and most civilized folk know of her (GM's discretion); the diva's modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +5." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Prima Donna (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-lower ability="As her efforts to outdo her rivals increase, a Chelish diva can spend additional rounds of bardic performance to augment *countersong, deadly performance, fascinate, frightening tune,* and *scathing tirade* (see below). When used, this ability gives her a +2 bonus on her Perform check or saving throw DC for those performances. The diva must spend an additional round of bardic performance for every round she uses *prima donna* to increase her Perform check result or saving throw DC." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Costume Proficiency (Ex)]{icon=def l5="The Chelish diva has become so used to wearing strange and cumbersome costumes for her performances that she gains medium armor proficiency and can cast bard spells while wearing medium armor without incurring the normal arcane spell failure chance." l11="She gains heavy armor proficiency and can cast bard spells while wearing heavy armor without incurring the normal arcane spell failure chance." special="A multiclassed diva still incurs the normal arcane spell failure chance for arcane spells received from other classes." replace="Lore master"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{icon=power head note="A Chelish diva gains the following bardic performances. This can never be performed more quickly than a @HLstandard_action."}

::ab[Devastating Aria (Su)]{next l=3 icon=zap standard="The Chelish diva can spend 1 round of bardic performance to direct a burst of sonically charged words at a creature or object. This performance deals 1d4 points of damage + the diva's level to an object, or half this damage to a living creature." replace="Inspire competence"}

:::ab{next l=8 title="Scathing Tirade (Su)" icon=lower action=Ability special="This performance cannot cause a creature to become ‹misc/panicked›, even if the target is already frightened from another effect. *Scathing tirade* is a mind-affecting fear effect, and it relies on audible and visual components." replace="Dirge of doom"}

A Chelish diva can use her performance to verbally lash out at another creature, causing it to become ‹misc/frightened›. To be affected, the target enemy must be within 30 feet and be able to see and hear the diva's performance. The effect persists for as long as the enemy is within 30 feet and the diva continues her performance, plus 1d4 rounds.

Although the diva can only direct the effect at one creature at a time, its effects persist for as long as she continues the performance, even if directed at a different creature. For example, she could direct her tirade at an innkeeper, who becomes frightened, then focus her wrath on the captain of the guard, and the innkeeper remains frightened for 1d4 more rounds even though her attention is no longer directed at him.

chronicler_of_worlds.name: Chronicler of Worlds
description: ## Chronicler of Worlds
‹SOURCE Planar Adventures/11›

In eons past, the angel Tabris was tasked with cataloging all knowledge of the Great Beyond, only to eventually be cast from Heaven for penning the ‹magic-artifact/Book of the Damned›, the perfect catalog of all planar evil. His dedication and sacrifice to the task have inspired some to follow his example; these bards are known as the chroniclers of worlds.

A chronicler of worlds forsakes morality in service of understanding; she is both warrior and mystic, scholar and soldier. Some chroniclers congregate in ‹rule/Axis› to share their discoveries and theories, but the majority wander the planes, steadfastly pushing the boundaries of what is known.

::ab[Wanderer's Insight]{icon=info note="A chronicler of worlds gains power not from her personality but from an analytical understanding of existence. She uses Intelligence instead of Charisma to determine the effects of bard class features, including spellcasting and bardic performance. A chronicler of worlds must have an alignment with at least one neutral component; a character who does not meet this requirement cannot use or benefit from this archetype's abilities."}

::ab[Planar Lore (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A chronicler of worlds can take 10 on Knowledge (planes) checks. A number of times per day equal to her Intelligence modifier, she can take 20 on a Knowledge (planes) check. This ability stacks with that gained by *lore master* at 5th level. A chronicler of worlds can attempt all Knowledge checks untrained." alter="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="The chronicler of worlds gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Quintessence Infusion (Su)]{next icon=power flavor="This performance infuses allies with planar quintessence." l9="While on a plane other than the Material Plane, the chronicler of worlds can use this performance to target an ally within 30 feet. Affected allies gain the plane's basic infusion effect (see ‹feat/Planar Infusion›)." l12="She can now affect two allies." l13="Affected creatures also gain the plane's improved infusion." l15="She can now affect three allies." l16="Affected creatures also gain the plane's greater infusion." l18="She can now affect four allies." special="The first time each day a creature benefits from quintessence infusion, the plane infused is set for the day and additional infusions can grant only that plane's infusions. Infusions that have limited uses per day remain limited in that way, regardless of how many times quintessence infusion is gained. Quintessence infusion relies on audible and visual components." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Mantra of Tabris (Su)]{next icon=def flavor="Tabris survived his travels through the darkest realms, his dedication to the pursuit of knowledge a shield against planar horrors. The chronicler of worlds walks in his footsteps." l=15 immediate="The chronicler of worlds can create a potent ward against outsiders. While maintaining this performance, when the chronicler of worlds is hit by an attack from an outsider or fails a saving throw against an outsider's extraordinary, supernatural, or spell-like ability, she can attempt a Knowledge (planes) check and use the result as her Armor Class or her saving throw result against that attack or effect. The chronicler cannot use her *planar lore* ability to take 20 on this Knowledge (planes) check. This can retroactively alter the results of the attack or ability." special="The chronicler of worlds must decide to use this ability before the effects of the successful attack or failed saving throw have been announced. Mantra of Tabris relies on audible components." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Amoral Scholar (Ex)]{icon=def l=2 passive="A chronicler of worlds rejects cosmic morality. She does not take penalties imposed by ‹rule/planar »alignment traits<_pa›, and she gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against spells and effects that vary based on their target's alignment." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Scrivener's Versatility (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost order=cp flavor="A chronicler of worlds seeks to emulate history's greatest scribe; studying the lore of Tabris grants insight into widely varying subjects, from anatomy to discourse." choice="A chronicler of worlds selects a skill from the following list: Bluff, Diplomacy, Disable Device, Heal, Intimidate, Profession (scribe), Sense Motive, or Survival." passive="When attempting skill checks of that type, a chronicler can use her Linguistics skill bonus in place of her bonus with the chosen skill." imp6="She selects a second skill to receive this benefit." imp10="She selects a third skill." imp14="She selects a fourth skill." imp18="She selects a fifth skill." replace="Versatile performance"}

court_bard.name: Court Bard
description: ## Court Bard
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/81›

Spending years studying all of the finer points of erudition and etiquette, the court bard takes up the role of resplendent proclaimer and artist-in-residence at the hand of nobility, royalty, and the well-moneyed elite who aspire to join their ranks.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A court bard gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Satire (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A court bard can use performance to undermine the confidence of enemies who hear it, causing them to take a -1 penalty on attack and damage rolls (minimum 1) and a -1 penalty on saves against fear and charm effects as long as the bard continues performing." imp5="The penalty becomes -2." imp11="The penalty becomes -3." imp17="The penalty becomes -4." special="*Satire* is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that uses audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Mockery (Su)]{next l=3 icon=lower flavor="A court bard can subtly ridicule and defame a specific individual." ability="The bard selects one target who can hear his performance. That individual takes a -2 penalty on Charisma checks and Charisma-related skill checks as long as the bard continues performing." imp7="The penalty becomes -3." imp11="The penalty becomes -4." imp15="The penalty becomes -5." imp19="The penalty becomes -6." special="*Mockery* is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Glorious Epic (Su)]{next icon=lower l=8 flavor="A court bard can weave captivating tales that engross those who hear them." ability="Enemies within 30 feet become ‹rule/flat-footed› unless they succeed at a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier). A save renders them immune to this ability for 24 hours." special="*Glorious epic* is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that uses audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Scandal (Su)]{next icon=lower l=14 flavor="A court bard can combine salacious gossip and biting calumny to incite a riot." ability="Each enemy within 30 feet is affected as if by a ‹spell/song of discord› for as long as it can hear the performance. A successful Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier) negates the effect, and that creature is immune to this ability for 24 hours." special="*Scandal* is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that uses audible components." replace="Frightening tune"}

::ab[Heraldic Expertise (Ex)]{icon=boost-roll passive="A court bard gains a bonus equal to half his bard level on Diplomacy, Knowledge (history), Knowledge (local), and Knowledge (nobility) checks (minimum +1)." ability="The court bard can also reroll a check against one of these skills, though he must take the result of the second roll even if it is worse." order=pau useInc="5~bard~+" useF="1~1~5~~5" replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Wide Audience (Su)]{icon=cone-boost ability="A court bard can choose to affect a 60-foot cone instead of a 30-foot radius with bardic performances that affect an area." imp10="The area of such powers is increased by 10 feet (radius) or 20 feet (cone). If the power instead affects multiple creatures, it affects one additional creature than normal." imp15="If the power instead affects multiple creatures, it affects two additional creatures than normal." imp20="If the power instead affects multiple creatures, it affects three additional creatures than normal." special="This does not affect powers that affect only a single creature." replace="Lore master~jack of all trades"}

court_fool.name: Court Fool
description: ## Court Fool
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/6›

Arguably the oldest and most widespread role for a court entertainer is that of the court jester or fool. This role arose from the need to offset the intense, even deadly, seriousness of court business. The court fool serves as an outlet for the constant pressure of aristocratic dealings, relieving tensions through humor. The motley clad acrobat is a good example of this style of court fool, using physical comedy to generate belly laughs and raise spirits. In other courts, the court fool might ridicule social conventions with bawdy songs or engage in biting satire, parodying manners or even mocking individuals to spread good cheer. The court fool holds up an unflattering mirror to those in charge or speaks truths, however veiled, that no one else can safely utter.
::ab[Buffoonery (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A court fool gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his bard level on Acrobatics, Bluff, Climb, and Disguise checks (minimum +1)." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A court fool gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Distracting Motley (Su)]{next l=1 icon=def-protect ability="A court fool can startle his allies back to their senses with erratic capering or by fluttering multicolored clothing. Each round, he attempts an Acrobatics check. Any ally (including the court fool) within 30 feet can use this check in place of her own saving throw against confusion and fascination effects. Those already under a confusion or fascination effect can attempt a new save each round using the court fool's Acrobatics check. A court fool wearing an ‹eq-misc/entertainer's outfit› gains a +2 bonus on these Acrobatics checks." special="*Distracting motley* doesn't work on effects that don't allow saves. This is a mind-affecting effect that uses visual components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Defuse Tension (Su)]{next l=3 icon=aid ability="A court fool can use a silly performance to comfort a beleaguered ally. That ally must be within 30 feet and be able to see the court fool. The ally ignores the ‹misc/fatigued› and ‹misc/shaken› conditions (but not more extreme conditions, such as exhausted or frightened) as long as she continues to see the court fool's performance." imp7="The court fool can affect two allies at once." imp11="The court fool can affect three allies." imp15="The court fool can affect four allies." imp19="The court fool can affect five allies." special="A court fool can't use *defuse tension* on himself. *Defuse tension* relies on visual components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Caper and Jeer (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost order=paw passive="A court fool can take 10 on Acrobatics and Bluff checks, even when in danger or distracted." ability="He can take 20 on an Acrobatics or Bluff check once per day, even when in danger or distracted." swift="A court fool can use Bluff to create a diversion to hide (as per the ‹skill/Stealth› skill)." imp11="He can take 20 as above twice per day." imp17="He can take 20 as above three times per day." replace="Lore master"}

cultivator.name: Cultivator
description: ## Cultivator
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/40›

Cultivators use music to promote plant growth and influence the behavior of plants both naturally and supernaturally. Most cultivators work in conjunction with local communities to maintain crops and oversee harvests, while others serve as wardens of unspoiled natural wonders.
::cskill{gain=sur}

::ab[Plant Magic]{icon=learn note="A cultivator bard adds the following spells to his bard spell list at the corresponding spell levels: ‹spell/entangle› (1st), ‹spell/greensight› (2nd), ‹spell/plant growth› (3rd), ‹spell/antiplant shell› (4th), ‹spell/tree stride› (5th), ‹spell/green caress› (6th)." alter="The bard's spells"}

::ab[Verdant Voice]{icon=power ability="By combining traditional ‹faith/Green Faith› chants and fey songs into his bardic performance, a cultivator gains the ability to affect a creature of the plant type with mind-affecting bardic performances. However, doing so requires the cultivator to expend 1 additional round of bardic performance each round he maintains a performance augmented by *verdant voice.*" replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Song of Growth (Su)]{icon=def-protect flavor="A cultivator can spontaneously promote massive plant growth with his bardic performance." standard="While he maintains his *song of growth,* the cultivator can select one side of a square within 30 feet of himself and instantly create an opaque barrier of plant matter to block that face, providing total ‹rule/cover›. He can create one barrier as part of starting his bardic performance if he starts the performance as a standard action. The side the cultivator selects must be supported by the ground, and the growth itself cannot support more than 5 pounds of weight. The conjured barrier has hardness 0, AC 5, and 2 hit points per bard level." special="A cultivator can maintain a number of plant growths equal to his Charisma modifier + ‹misc/half› his bard level. These barriers immediately crumble to dust when the cultivator ends his performance." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Resist Nature's Lure (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-def passive="A cultivator gains ‹ability/resist nature's lure›, as the druid class feature of the same name, but with only a +2 bonus instead of +4." imp4="This increases to a +4 bonus." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Nature Lore (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-down ability="The cultivator gains the *lore master* bard ability, except he can use it only on Knowledge (nature) checks and Survival checks to track creatures in natural environments." alter="Lore master"}

daredevil.name: Daredevil
description: ## Daredevil
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/32›

As quick at wordplay as at swordplay, daredevils are dashing heroes, inspiring their allies to match their clever repartee and acrobatic feats. Daredevils often lean toward comedy, dance, oratory, and singing.
::ab[Agile (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A daredevil adds ‹misc/half› her class level (minimum 1) on Acrobatics, Bluff, Climb, and Escape Artist checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A daredevil gains the following type of bardic performance."}

::ab[Derring-do (Su)]{next icon=def-protect-boost flavor="A daredevil can use her performance to bring out grace in the clumsiest allies." ability="The daredevil and any allies who see her receive a +1 morale bonus on Reflex saving throws and a +2 competence bonus on Dexterity-based skill checks. Allies who move at least 10 feet during their turn gain a +1 dodge bonus to their Armor Class until the start of their next turn." imp5="The skill bonus becomes +4, and the other bonuses become +2." imp11="The skill bonus becomes +6, and the other bonuses become +3." imp17="The skill bonus becomes +8, and the other bonuses become +4." special="This is a mind-affecting ability that uses visual components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Canny Foe (Ex)]{l=2 icon=melee-def choice="A daredevil can choose one type of ‹rule/combat maneuver<s›." passive="She gains a +2 bonus on her combat maneuver checks to attempt the chosen maneuver and to her CMD to resist that maneuver." order=cp imp6="The daredevil gains a +2 bonus for a second type of combat maneuver." imp10="The daredevil chooses a third combat maneuver to gain a +2 bonus." imp14="The daredevil chooses a fourth combat maneuver to gain a +2 bonus." imp18="The daredevil chooses a fifth combat maneuver to gain a +2 bonus." special="She may not choose the same maneuver twice." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Dauntless (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A daredevil gains a +1 morale bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects, including all fear effects." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Scoundrel's Fortune (Ex)]{icon=roll useInc="3~bard~5" useF="5~1~3" l=5 ability="A daredevil can choose to roll two dice instead of one for any skill check, keeping the best result." replace="Lore master"}

dawnflower_dervish.name: Dawnflower Dervish
description: ## Dawnflower Dervish
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Magic/34›

Although Sarenrae is seen mainly as a goddess of healing and redemption in most parts of the Inner Sea, her stern, evil-smiting element is more common in areas like Qadira, Osirion, and Katapesh. Many of the Dawnflower's disciples from these lands become Dawnflower dervishes, religious mystics who use a spinning dance as part of their worship. Many bards of her faith hone their skills with dance and scimitar to become ‹arc-bard/dervish dancer«s›, but some tread a similar path focused more on magic and healing than swordplay. These are the Dawnflower dervishes. A Dawnflower dervish has the following class features.
::ab[Deity]{icon=info note="A Dawnflower dervish must be a worshiper of ‹faith/Sarenrae›. A dervish who abandons or betrays this faith reverts to a standard bard."}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power ability="Dawnflower dervishes gain weapon proficiency with the ‹eq-weapon/scimitar›." replace="Proficiency with the rapier and whip"}

:::ab{title="Battle Dance" icon=power action=Ability imp10="A Dawnflower dervish can start a battle dance as a @HLswift_action instead of a move action." alter="Bardic performance" special="When the Dawnflower dervish uses the *inspire courage, inspire greatness,* or *inspire heroics* bardic performance types as battle dances, these performance types only provide benefit to the Dawnflower dervish himself. All other types of bardic performance work normally (affecting the bard and his allies, or the bard's enemies, as appropriate)."}

A Dawnflower dervish is trained in the use of the Perform skill, especially dance, to create magical effects on himself. This works like bardic performance, except that the Dawnflower dervish's performances grant double their normal bonuses, but these bonuses only affect him. He does not need to be able to see or hear his own performance. Battle dancing is treated as bardic performance for the purposes of feats, abilities, and effects that affect bardic performance, except that battle dancing does not benefit from the ‹feat/Lingering Performance› feat or any other ability that allows a bardic performance to grant bonuses after it has ended. The benefits of battle dancing apply only when the bard is wearing light or no armor. Like bardic performance, battle dancing cannot be maintained at the same time as other performance abilities.

Starting a battle dance is a @HL[move action], but it can be maintained each round as a @HL[free action]. Changing a battle dance from one effect to another requires the Dawnflower dervish to stop the previous performance and start the new one as a move action. Like a bard, a Dawnflower dervish's performance ends immediately if he is killed, paralyzed, stunned, knocked unconscious, or otherwise prevented from taking a free action each round. A Dawnflower dervish cannot perform more than one battle dance at a time.
::ab[Dervish Dance (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A Dawnflower dervish gains the ‹feat/Dervish Dance› feat as a bonus feat." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Spinning Spellcaster (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="A Dawnflower dervish gains a +4 bonus on ‹rule/concentration› checks to cast spells defensively." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Meditative Whirl (Ex)]{l=8 icon=magic-aid flavor="When using battle dance, the Dawnflower dervish can enter a trance-like state where his spinning motion represents the movement of the planets around the sun, and his spirit is attuned to the healing aspects of Sarenrae." useInc="2~dervish~8" useF="8~1~2" move="By focusing on his whirling, the dervish can apply the ‹feat/Quicken Spell› feat to any ‹main/cure spell<s› he is about to cast (effectively spending a move action and swift action to cast the spell). This does not alter the level of the spell or the casting time." replace="Dirge of doom"}

demagogue.name: Demagogue
description: ## Demagogue
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/26›

Not content with providing amusing and occasionally instructive performances, the demagogue seeks to inflame and ignite his audience, driving them toward a specific purpose with carefully chosen words and tones that may spark momentous change.

::ab[Famous]{l=1 icon=boost choice="A demagogue may choose a region where he is famous, and within that region, the locals are more likely to react favorably toward him. The bard gains a bonus on Bluff and Intimidate checks in that area and to influence people from that area." imp1="This region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of 1,000 or fewer people, and the modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +1. As the demagogue grows more famous, additional areas learn of him (typically places where he has lived or traveled, or settlements adjacent to those where he is known) and his bonuses apply to even more people." imp5="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of 5,000 or fewer people, and the modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +2." imp9="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of up to 25,000 people, and the modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +3." imp13="The region is a settlement or settlements with a total population of up to 100,000 people, and the modifier to Bluff and Intimidate is +4." imp17="The demagogue's renown has spread far, and most civilized folk know of him (GM's discretion); the demagogue's modifier on Bluff and Intimidate checks is +5." replace="Inspire courage +1"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A demagogue gains the following type of bardic performance."}

::ab[Gather Crowd (Ex)]{next l=5 icon=power ability="The demagogue is skilled at drawing an audience to his performances. If he is in a settlement or populated area, he can shout, sing, or otherwise make himself noticed in order to attract an audience to his impromptu stage. The size of the crowd depends on the local population, but typically is a number of people equal to ‹misc/half› the bard's class level &times& the result of the bard's Perform check. The crowd gathers over the next 1d10 rounds." special="If the bard fails to engage the crowd (such as by performing, kissing babies, trying to use *fascinate,* and so on), it disperses over the next 1d10 rounds." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Incite Violence (Ex)]{next l=6 icon=power ability="The demagogue can use his performance to fan the fury of a crowd of people he has fascinated. Using this ability does not disrupt the *fascinate* effect, but does require a @HLstandard_action to activate (in addition to the @HLfree_action to continue the *fascinate* effect). The bard selects a number of targets equal to his level, who must make Will saves (DC 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) or be affected by ‹spell/rage› for a number of rounds equal to the bard's level. The bard indicates who is the intended target of violence (either after using this ability or as part of the performance leading to it) and the enraged members of the crowd immediately attack the target if possible. The target does not need to be present (&quot&kill the king&quot& is a suitable choice) and can be an object instead of a person (&quot&destroy the prison!&quot& is likewise appropriate). Other members of the crowd may follow suit, though they do not gain the benefits of *rage.*" special="This is a sound-based effect and is affected by *countersong.* If two or more bards are attempting to direct the crowd against different targets, they must make opposed Charisma checks, with the crowd following the directions of the winner." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Righteous Cause (Ex)]{next l=18 icon=power ability="The demagogue can lift a crowd's emotions and turn them toward a common purpose. First, he must *fascinate* the crowd, and then use *incite violence* without designating a target, at which point he can use *righteous cause.* Instead of driving the crowd with anger, he fills them with purpose. Fascinated creatures must make Will saves (DC 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) to resist. Those who fail are affected by ‹spell/mass suggestion› of a plausible idea that lingers with them for one day. Typical uses of this ability are to spark rebellion, overthrow a king, build a beneficial structure such as an orphanage, or donate money to a cause." replace="Mass suggestion"}

dervish_dancer.name: Dervish Dancer
description: ## Dervish Dancer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/32›
Not all bards inspire others with their performances. Dervish dancers enter a near-mystical trance that allows them to push their bodies beyond normal limits.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down ability="Dervish dancers gain weapon proficiency with the ‹eq-weapon/scimitar› and ‹eq-weapon/kukri›." replace="Proficiency with the rapier and whip"}

:::ab{title="Battle Dance" head icon=power action=Ability imp10="A dervish dancer can start a *battle dance* as a @HLswift_action instead of a move action." alter="Bardic performance" special="Dervish dancers gain the *inspire courage, inspire greatness,* and *inspire heroics* bardic performance types as *battle dances,* but these only provide benefit to the dervish dancer himself."}

A dervish dancer is trained in the use of the Perform skill, especially dance, to create magical effects on himself. This works like bardic performance, except the dervish dancer only affects himself, and does not need to be able to see or hear his own performance. Battle dancing is treated as bardic performance for the purposes of feats, abilities, effects, and the like that affect bardic performance, except that *battle dancing* does not benefit from the ‹feat/Lingering Performance› feat or any other ability that allows a bardic performance to grant bonuses after it has ended. Battle dancing benefits apply only when the bard is wearing light or no armor. Like bardic performance, it cannot be maintained at the same time as other performance abilities.

Starting a *battle dance* is a @HL[move action], but it can be maintained each round as a @HL[free action]. Changing a *battle dance* from one effect to another requires the dervish dancer to stop the previous performance and start the new one as a move action. Like a bard, a dervish dancer's performance ends immediately if he is killed, paralyzed, stunned, knocked unconscious, or otherwise prevented from taking a free action each round. A dervish dancer cannot perform more than one *battle dance* at a time.

::ab[Rain of Blows (Su)]{next icon=boost-def l=6 ability="A dervish dancer can use his *battle dance* to speed up his attacks. When making a ‹rule/full attack› action, he may make one extra attack with any weapon he is holding, as though under the effects of a ‹spell/haste› spell. He also gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls and a +1 dodge bonus to AC and on Reflex saves." increment="p!The bonuses~6~3" special="These bonuses do not stack with the *haste* spell." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion"}

::ab[Razor's Kiss (Su)]{next icon=boost l=8 passive="A dervish dancer can use his *battle dance* to improve his weapons' critical range. All attacks he makes with manufactured weapons are treated as though he had the ‹feat/Improved Critical› feat. Natural weapons and spells are not affected." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Leaf on the Wind (Su)]{next l=14 icon=def ability="A dervish dancer can use his *battle dance* to evade attacks with unearthly grace and to shake off the effects of his wounds. Unlike other *battle dances, leaf on the wind* requires a @HLstandard_action each round to start or maintain the performance. Each round it is maintained, including the first, the dervish dancer gains a +6 dodge bonus to Armor Class and on Reflex saves. If wounded, he heals 1 hit point of damage per bard level." replace="Frightening performance"}

::ab[Fleet (Su)]{icon=boost passive="While performing a *battle dance,* a dervish dancer gains a +10 enhancement bonus to his land speed." increment="This bonus~1~4~15~5" incrementEnd=" feet." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}

::ab[Versatile Dance (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="At 2nd level, a dervish dancer gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his level on Perform (dance) checks." order=pa ability="He can use his bonus for his Perform (dance) skill in place of his bonus for Acrobatics." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Dance of Fury (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee-boost ability="A dervish dancer can attack more than once as he moves while performing a *battle dance.* He can combine a full-attack action with a single move, taking the attacks at any point during his movement, but must move at least 5 feet between each attack. This movement provokes attacks of opportunity as normal." replace="Soothing performance"}

::ab[Battle Fury (Su)]{l=20 icon=melee-boost flavor="The dervish dancer can unleash a whirlwind of blows while performing a *battle dance.*" fullround="He can take a single move action and unleash a single attack at his highest bonus against each target within his reach during any point of his move, up to a maximum number of attacks equal to the dervish dancer's character level. This movement provokes attacks of opportunity as normal." replace="Deadly performance"}

detective.name: Detective
description: ## Detective
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/81›
Piecing together clues and catching the guilty with sheer cleverness, the detective is skilled at divining the truth.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A detective gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Careful Teamwork (Su)]{next icon=boost-def ability="A detective uses performance to keep allies coordinated, alert, and ready for action. All allies within 30 feet gain a +1 bonus on Initiative checks, Perception, and Disable Device checks for 1 hour. They also gain a +1 insight bonus on Reflex saves and to AC against traps and when they are ‹rule/flat-footed›." increment="p!The bonuses~-1~6" special="Using this ability requires 3 rounds of continuous performance, and the targets must be able to see and hear the bard throughout the performance. This ability is language-dependent and requires visual and audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[True Confession (Su)]{next icon=lower l=9 ability="A detective can use performance to trick a creature into revealing its secrets. Using this ability requires a successful Sense Motive check to see through a Bluff or notice mental compulsion. After 3 continuous rounds of performance, the target must make a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier). Success renders the target immune to this power for 24 hours. On a failed save, a liar inadvertently reveals the lie and the truth behind it. A creature under a charm or compulsion reveals the nature of its enchantment and who placed it (if the creature knows) and gains a new saving throw to break free from the enchantment." special="This ability is language-dependent and requires audible components." imp15="Using this power requires only 2 rounds of performance." imp20="This power now only requires 1 round of performance." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Show Yourselves (Ex)]{next icon=lower l=15 ability="A detective can use performance to compel creatures to reveal themselves when hiding. All enemies within 30 feet must make a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier). If they fail, they must cease using Stealth, unlock and open doors between themselves and the detective, and dismiss, suppress, or dispel if necessary magical effects that grant ‹spell/invisibility› or any other form of ‹rule/concealment› from the detective. As long as they can hear the performance, affected creatures may not attack or flee until they have eliminated every such effect, though they are freed from this compulsion immediately if attacked." special="Creatures in the area must make this save each round the bard continues his performance. This ability is language-dependent and requires audible components." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Eye for Detail (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A detective gains a bonus equal to half his level on Knowledge (local), Perception, and Sense Motive checks, as well as Diplomacy checks to gather information (minimum +1)." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Arcane Insight (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-def ability="A detective can find and disable magical traps, like a rogue's ‹ability/trapfinding› ability. In addition, he gains a +4 bonus on saving throws made against illusions and a +4 bonus on caster level checks and saving throws to see through disguises and protections against divination (such as ‹spell/magic aura›, ‹spell/misdirection›, and ‹spell/nondetection›)." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Arcane Investigation]{icon=learn note="In addition, a detective's class spell list includes the following: 1st-‹spell/detect chaos›/‹spell/detect_>evil›/‹spell/detect_>law›/‹spell/detect_>good›; 2nd-‹spell/zone of truth›; 3rd-‹spell/arcane eye›, ‹spell/speak with dead›, ‹spell/speak with plants›; 4th-‹spell/discern lies›; 5th-‹spell/prying eyes›, ‹spell/stone tell›; 6th-‹spell/discern location›, ‹spell/find the path›, ‹spell/greater prying eyes›, ‹spell/moment of prescience›." imp2="A detective may add one of these spells or any divination spell on the bard spell list to his list of spells known at 2nd level and every four levels thereafter (6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels)." replace="Versatile performance"}

dirge_bard.name: Dirge Bard
description: ## Dirge Bard
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/26›

A composer of sonorous laments for the dead and elaborate requiems for those lost yet long remembered, dirge bards master musical tools and tropes that must appeal to the ears and hearts of both the living and the dead.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A dirge bard gains the following type of bardic performance."}

::ab[Dance of the Dead (Su)]{next icon=power ability="A dirge bard can use his bardic performance to cause dead bones or bodies to rise up and move or fight at his command. This ability functions like ‹spell/animate dead›, but the created skeletons or zombies remain fully animate only as long as the dirge bard continues the performance. Once it stops, any created undead collapse into carrion. Bodies or bones cannot be animated more than once using this ability. Unlike *animate dead,* dance of the dead requires no components and does not have the evil descriptor." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}
::ab[Haunted Eyes (Ex)]{icon=def l=2 passive="A dirge bard gains a +4 bonus on saves against fear, energy drain, death effects, and necromantic effects." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Secrets of the Grave (Ex)]{icon=boost l=2 passive="A dirge bard gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his bard level on Knowledge (religion) checks made to identify undead creatures and their abilities." ability="A dirge bard may use mind-affecting spells to affect undead as if they were living creatures, even if they are mindless (though spells that affect only humanoids do not affect them, even if they were humanoids in life)." order=pa special="In addition, he may add one ‹main/spells_>necromancy« spell› from the spell list of any arcane spellcasting class to his list of spells known at 2nd level and every four levels thereafter (6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels)." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Haunting Refrain (Su)]{l=5 icon=lower ability="A dirge bard is able to stir primal terrors in the hearts of listeners. He can use a Perform (keyboard) or Perform (percussion) check in place of an Intimidate check to demoralize an opponent, with a bonus equal to half his bard level. In addition, saving throws against any fear effect he creates are made with a -2 penalty." imp10="The penalty becomes -3." imp15="The penalty becomes -4." imp20="The penalty becomes -5." replace="Lore master"}

disciple_of_the_forked_tongue.name: Disciple of the Forked Tongue
description: ## Disciple of the Forked Tongue
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/7›
