Studied in venomous words as well as venomous blood, a disciple of the forked tongue knows just the right phrase to slowly poison the soul. His voice can sink deep into his victims' hearts, destroying friendships and devouring the mind.
::prereq{r=Vishkanya}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A disciple of the forked tongue gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Discordant Spiral (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="The disciple can disrupt the thought processes of enemies who hear his performance, causing them to take a -1 penalty on saves against mind-affecting and curse effects and a -2 penalty on ‹rule/concentration› checks." imp5="These penalties become -2 and -3, respectively." imp11="These penalties become -3 and -4." imp17="These penalties become -4 and -5." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Venomous Whispers (Su)]{l=9 icon=lower ability="A disciple of the forked tongue can use his performance to sow distrust into the mind of an enemy within 30 feet. An affected creature treats its allies as hostile for the purposes of their spells and abilities - it is never considered a willing target of spells and must attempt saving throws against them when possible, and it gains no benefit from allied bardic performances and other abilities that specifically target allies." imp12="He can now affect two creatures at the same time." imp15="He can now affect three creatures at the same time." imp18="He can now affect four creatures at the same time." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Serpent of the Mind (Su)]{icon=learn l2="A disciple of the forked tongue can add one spell with the ‹spelldef/curse› descriptor from the spell list of any spellcasting class to his list of spells known." l6="He adds another *curse* spell to his list of spells known." l10="He adds another *curse* spell." l14="He adds another *curse* spell." l18="He adds another *curse* spell." replace="Versatile performance"}

dragon_herald.name: Dragon Herald
description: ## Dragon Herald
‹SOURCE Kobolds of Golarion/22›

Dragon heralds are messengers and evangelists of true dragonkind. They carry the word of their dragon patrons far and wide, whether that's a message of peace or a declaration of war. A kobold whose scales already associate her with a revered dragon is often trained as her tribe's dragon herald.
::prereq{r=Kobold}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down ability="The dragon herald is proficient with simple weapons and light armor, but not with shields. Dragon heralds are messengers, not warriors. Like other bards, dragon heralds don't incur the normal arcane spell failure chance from wearing light armor."}

::ab[Dragon Patron (Ex)]{icon=info choice="A dragon herald must choose a particular ‹family/true dragon› species whose glory to extol. In most cases, this is a dragon with whom the herald is familiar, but it could be a dragon deity, a long-dead dragon hero, or even an entire dragon lineage. The energy type associated with her dragon patron (as shown on the ‹sorcbloodline/draconic« sorcerer bloodline›) determines the energy type used by several of her abilities. She must also designate a sacred space relevant to her dragon choice - usually the lair of a patron dragon or a shrine or holy place associated with a dragon type."}

::ab[Coat of Arms (Ex)]{icon=def passive="A dragon herald gains ‹umr/energy »resistance› equal to her dragon herald level against the energy type associated with her dragon patron. This resistance stacks with any inherent racial resistances, but not with magical or temporary resistances."}

::ab[Dragon Voice (Ex)]{icon=boost-power passive="A dragon herald gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› her level on Intimidate and Diplomacy checks. She gains Draconic as a bonus language." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A dragon herald gains the following types of bardic performance:"}

::ab[Diplomatic Immunity (Su)]{next icon=def ability="A dragon herald can use bardic performance to generate a ‹spell/sanctuary› effect upon herself. Most use this to deliver the word of their patron or to safely complete Diplomacy or Intimidate checks in the presence of hostile foes. The effect persists for as long as the dragon herald performs and for 1 round after." replace=Countersong~fascinate}

::ab[Diplomatic Protection (Su)]{next l=3 icon=protect ability="The dragon herald can use bardic performance to wrap a single ally in the colors and scales of her patron. The dragon herald must be able to see her ally, and that ally must be able to perceive her performance. While the herald performs, the target gains resistance equal to twice the dragon herald's level to the energy type associated with the dragon herald's patron, and an enhancement bonus to natural armor equal to 1/2 the dragon herald's level." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Rebuke Foes (Su)]{next l=12 icon=zap ability="The dragon herald gains the ability to rain down her patron's wrath on her enemies, dealing energy damage of the appropriate type equal to twice her dragon herald level (Reflex save for half). She can target one enemy within 50 feet for every 4 dragon herald levels she has. This effect occurs once per round for as long as she continues her performance." replace="Soothing performance"}

::ab[Retreat to Lair (Sp)]{next l=15 icon=magic fullround="The dragon herald can spend 5 daily uses of performance to teleport herself, or a willing target touched, to her sacred place." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Master of Persuasion (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="The dragon herald becomes so sure in her craft that she can take 10 on any Diplomacy or Intimidate check, even when rushed or threatened." order=ar fullround="Once per day, instead of spending a full minute, she may attempt either a Diplomacy or Intimidate check." imp11="You can use the full-round ability twice a day." imp17="You can use the full-round ability three times a day." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Extol Glory (Su)]{l=10 icon=power passive="The dragon herald gains the ability to spread her patron's glory to all intelligent creatures. When she speaks Draconic, any intelligent creature that hears her and knows any language can understand her words." special="This ability does not allow the dragon herald to understand languages with which she is not familiar." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}

dragon_yapper.name: Dragon Yapper
description: ## Dragon Yapper
‹SOURCE Monster Codex/128›
Kobolds' yammering songs distract opponents in combat, hindering their ability to attack.
::prereq{r=Kobold}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A dragon yapper gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Yapping Song (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A dragon yapper can use performance to annoy those that hear it, causing them to take a -1 penalty on attack and damage rolls (minimum 1) and a -1 penalty on saves against fear effects and charm effects as long as the dragon yapper continues performing." imp5="These penalties become -2." imp11="These penalties become -3." imp17="These penalties become -4." special="Yapping song is a mind-affecting ability that uses audible components, but is not language-dependent." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Frightful Song (Su)]{next l=8 icon=lower-aura ability="A dragon yapper can amplify his voice to sound like an actual dragon. Enemies within 30 feet become ‹misc/shaken› unless they succeed at a Will save (DC 10 +‹misc/half› the dragon yapper's level + the dragon yapper's Charisma modifier). A successful save renders targets immune to this ability for 24 hours." special="Frightful song is a mind-affecting ability, and a fear effect that uses audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}
::ab[Versatile Performance (Ex)]{icon=info l2="A dragon yapper must choose sing at 2nd level."}

duettist.name: Duettist
description: ## Duettist
‹SOURCE Familiar Folio/6›

Whether singing a delicate duo with a nightingale or slipping coins from purses while a trained monkey distracts the crowd, the duettist blends his bond to his familiar with his natural talent for performance to create amazing effects.
::ab[Diminished Competency]{icon=down note="A duettist does not gain the *well-versed* or *jack-of-all-trades* class features."}
::ab[Familiar (Ex)]{icon=power l1="A duettist gains a ‹sidekick/familiar›, treating his bard level as his effective wizard level for the purpose of this ability." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Versatile Familiar (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="A duettist's familiar learns to use performance to supplement its skills." l2="A duettist's familiar benefits from its master's *versatile performance* class feature." alter="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Performing Familiar (Su)]{icon=power flavor="A duettist's familiar learns how to create supernatural effects with its performances, just like its master." l4="The familiar can use any of its master's bardic performances, but only the familiar or the duettist can have a performance active at any given time, not both. If one is performing and the other starts a performance, the previous performance immediately ends." special="Each round that the familiar performs consumes 2 rounds of the duettist's bardic performance." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Harmonizing Familiar (Su)]{icon=boost flavor="The duettist and his familiar have learned how to perform together in harmony." l8="When a duettist and his familiar perform the same bardic performance simultaneously, its effects are enhanced. If the performance has a DC, the DC increases by 2. If the performance provides a competence bonus, the competence bonus increases by 1." special="Because both the duettist and the familiar are performing, each round performed consumes 3 rounds of bardic performance." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Symphonic Familiar (Su)]{icon=power flavor="The duettist and his familiar have learned how to create a symphony of complementary performances that meld together to produce two different effects." l14="They can each perform a different bardic performance simultaneously, and each has its full effect." special="Because both the duettist and the familiar are performing, each round performed consumes 3 rounds of bardic performance." replace="Frightening tune"}

dwarven_scholar.name: Dwarven Scholar
description: ## Dwarven Scholar
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/30›
A dwarven scholar researches the lineage of the kings of the old dwarven empires and learns their ancient tactics. She keeps these ancient traditions alive by sharing them with her allies.
::prereq{other="This archetype appears to be intended for ‹race/dwarves› only, but that limitation is never explicitly stated."}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down note="A dwarven scholar is proficient with one weapon of her choosing with the word &quot&dwarven&quot& in its name, in addition to her normal weapon proficiencies. She is not proficient with shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Studied Insight (Ex)]{icon=info note="A dwarven scholar uses her Wisdom modifier instead of her Charisma modifier to determine all class features and effects relating to her bard class, including her bardic performance ability, ‹rule/bonus spells› per day, the maximum spell level she can cast, and the save DCs of her spells."}

::ab[Dwarven Training (Ex)]{icon=power l2="A dwarven scholar masters one aspect of dwarven combat and gains a bonus ‹main/combat feat›. She must meet the prerequisites for this feat, but she treats her bard level as her base attack bonus (in addition to base attack bonuses gained from other classes and racial Hit Dice) for the purpose of qualifying for this feat." l6="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." l10="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." l14="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." l18="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A dwarven scholar gains the following type of bardic performance."}

::ab[War Chant (Su)]{next icon=boost l1="A dwarven scholar can use her performance to inspire the martial prowess of the ancient dwarves in her allies. The dwarven scholar selects a single combat feat she knows. Allies affected by this performance gain the benefit of this feat for as long as the performance is maintained. To be affected, an ally must be able to perceive the dwarven scholar's performance. If a combat feat has a daily use limitation (such as ‹feat/Stunning Fist›), any uses of that combat feat by the dwarven scholar's allies while using this ability counts toward the dwarven scholar's feat's daily limit." l6="The dwarven scholar may grant the benefit of two combat feats at the same time. She may use one of these feats to meet a prerequisite of the second feat. Maintaining two feats in this way requires spending 2 rounds of bardic performance every round." replace="Inspire courage~suggestion"}

faith_singer.name: Faith Singer
description: ## Faith Singer
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/19›
Faith singers show their faith in their deity with songs or other appropriate performances.
::ab[Faithful]{icon=info note="Like a cleric, a faith singer must worship a ‹main/deity»<faiths›, and his alignment must be within one step of his deity's alignment."}

::ab[Devout Spell Knowledge (Su)]{icon=power-magic l2="A faith singer can select one ‹ability/domain<s› from among those belonging to his deity. Once per day as a spell-like ability using his bard level as his caster level, he can cast the 1st-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l6="He can similarly cast the 2nd-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l10="He can similarly cast the 3rd-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l14="He can similarly cast the 4th-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l18="He can similarly cast the 5th-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." special="He can use each spell-like ability only once per day." replace="Versatile performance"}

fey_courtier.name: Fey Courtier
description: ## Fey Courtier
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/11›
Fey who associate with courts often become bards skilled at navigating the inhuman societies of the trackless wilds.
::prereq{r=Gathlain}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="The fey courtier has access to the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Scorn of the Wilds (Sp)]{next l=8 icon=magic ability="The fey courtier can use bardic performance to permanently mark a creature with nature's displeasure by spending 2 rounds performing while the target remains within 30 feet. The performance includes a litany of the target's misdeeds against fey and nature. Unless the target succeeds at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + his Charisma modifier), it is cursed as per ‹spell/nature's exile› or ‹spell/bestow curse›." imp14="The fey courtier's options expand to include ‹spell/baleful polymorph› and ‹spell/green caress›." special="As part of the performance, the fey courtier must define a condition under which the curse will be lifted, which must relate to making amends for the crime against fey or nature and cannot be suicidal to carry out. A creature that succeeds at its saving throw is immune to the fey courtier's scorn of the wild for 24 hours. This is a curse effect." replace="Dirge of doom~frightening tune"}

::ab[Stone Dance (Sp)]{next l=15 icon=magic ability="The bard's performance can move even the features of the natural world. This performance functions as ‹spell/animate plants› (DC = 10 + half the bard's level + his Charisma modifier) except it can also animate unworked stone and water from natural bodies of water (with statistics equivalent to an appropriate plant)." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Fey Contacts]{l=2 icon=power-boost passive="Thanks to his fey contacts and knowledge of the byzantine trade customs of the fey courts, a fey courtier can treat any wilderness area with fey residents as a settlement with a certain base value and purchase limit based on his bard level for the purpose of buying and selling magic items (see the table below). The fey courtier can also use these contacts to gather information about the residents, geography, and nearby areas with Diplomacy." replace="Versatile performance"}
These rules come from ‹rule/The Settlement Stat Block›:

>*There is a 75% chance that any item of [the Base Value] or lower can be found for sale in the community with little effort. If an item is not available, a new check to determine if the item has become available can be made in 1 week. A settlement's purchase limit is the most money a shop in the settlement can spend to purchase any single item from the PCs. If the PCs wish to sell an item worth more than a settlement's purchase limit, they'll either need to settle for a lower price, travel to a larger city, or (with the GM's permission) search for a specific buyer in the city with deeper pockets.*
| Bard Level | Base Value | Purchase Limit |

::ab[Summon Fey Allies]{l=3 icon=learn-power ability="The fey courtier gains ‹feat/Augment Summoning› as a bonus feat and can call upon natural allies who owe him favors. He adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› to his list of 1st-level bard spells known. Whenever he gains access to another spell level, he adds the next-higher *summon nature's ally* spell to his list of bard spells known." special="The fey courtier adds the following creatures to the *summon nature's ally* list: 1st-‹monster/grimple« (gremlin)›, ‹monster/mite›, ‹monster/sprite›; 2nd-‹monster/atomie›, ‹monster/fuath« (gremlin)›, ‹monster/nixie›; 3rd-‹monster/leprechaun›, ‹monster/nuglub« (gremlin)›, ‹monster/pooka›; 4th-‹monster/kelpie›, ‹monster/korred›, ‹monster/twigjack›; 5th-‹monster/lampad›, ‹monster/lurker in light›, ‹monster/swan maiden›; 6th-‹monster/cold rider›, ‹monster/oceanid›, ‹monster/sangoi›." replace="Inspire competence"}

fey_prankster.name: Fey Prankster
description: ## Fey Prankster
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/20›
Sly and mischievous like the Lantern King himself, fey pranksters learn supernatural tricks that can cause their victims to suffer mishaps and accidents.

::ab[Mischievous Talent (Ex)]{icon=boost-power passive="A fey prankster adds ‹misc/half› her class level (minimum 1) on Bluff, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth skill checks, and can attempt Sleight of Hand checks untrained." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A fey prankster gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Song of Clumsiness (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A fey prankster can use this performance to cause enemies within 30 feet that can hear her to suffer seemingly random mishaps. Any enemy in this area that draws a weapon or retrieves an item from a backpack or similar container immediately drops it, and falls prone the first time it enters a square of ‹rule/difficult terrain› during its turn. A creature can negate a specific effect with a successful Reflex saving throw (DC = 10 + half the fey prankster's bard level + her Charisma modifier), but an enemy must attempt a separate save for each potential effect during the song of clumsiness (for example, if it draws multiple weapons)." special="Song of clumsiness is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting ability that relies on audible components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Incite Unreliability (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A fey prankster can use her performance to manipulate a single target into fighting erratically or haphazardly, as though affected by ‹spell/lesser confusion›. To be affected, the target must be within 30 feet and able to hear the fey prankster's performance. The effect persists for as long as the target can hear the performance. A target that succeeds at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half the fey prankster's bard level + her Charisma modifier) negates the effect and becomes immune to incite unreliability from the same fey prankster for 24 hours." special="Incite unreliability is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting ability that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Embarrassing Satire (Su)]{next icon=zap l=8 ability="A fey prankster's powerful satire raises painful blisters and ugly boils on the face of her target. The fey prankster selects one target within 30 feet to become ‹misc/sickened› from the uncomfortable facial boils. The sickened effect continues for as long as the target is within 30 feet and the fey prankster continues the performance, though the boils remain for 1 day afterward." special="Embarrassing satire is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that relies on audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Dirty Trickster (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A fey prankster gains ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat and counts as having ‹feat/Combat Expertise› for the purpose of meeting the prerequisites of feats that have Improved Dirty Trick as a prerequisite." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Master of Mischief (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost ability="A fey prankster can take 10 on a Bluff, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth skill check (if she has ranks in that skill), even if distracted or endangered. In addition, once per day, the fey prankster can take 20 on one these skill checks (if she has ranks in that skill)." useInc="6~bard~5" useF="5~1~6" replace="Lore master"}

filidh.name: Filidh
description: ## Filidh
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/40›
By tapping into the world's natural music, specialized bards known as filidhs are able to see not only the tapestry of life but divine portents of the future from the rhythm of all life's song.

::ab[Armor and Weapon Proficiency]{icon=down note="A filidh is proficient with light armor and is prohibited from wearing metal armor. A filidh can wear wooden armor that has been altered by the ‹spell/ironwood› spell. Filidhs are proficient with shields (except tower shields) but can use only those made of wood." special="A filidh who wears prohibited armor or uses a prohibited shield is unable to cast bard spells or use any of his supernatural or spell-like class abilities while doing so and for 24 hours thereafter." alter="Armor and weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Natural Magic]{icon=magic note="A filidh casts spells as a bard, but the spells are divine, not arcane, and therefore not subject to arcane spell failure. A filidh must use a holy symbol or a musical instrument as a divine focus when a spell includes such a component. He still uses the bard spell list." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Nature's Song]{icon=magic useL=bard useUnit=time ability="Filidhs can hear the resonant song generated by all living creatures - a primal music that stretches back to the beginning of time and ahead to the unwritten future. By tapping into this cosmic melody, a filidh can steal glimpses of the future." order=awu swift="A filidh can sacrifice a spell slot to gain an additional number of rounds of bardic performance for that day equal to the spell's level."}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A filidh gains the following types of bardic performances."}

::ab[Echoes of Nature's Song (Su)]{next icon=protect ability="A filidh can use his bardic performance to imbue his allies with subconscious knowledge of their own futures, improving their reflexes and ability to avoid danger. An affected ally receives a +1 insight bonus on Reflex saving throws and to AC." increment="This bonus~-1~6" special="This performance is a mind-affecting ability and relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Divinatory Song (Sp)]{next l=6 icon=magic ability="A filidh can use his performance to create an effect equivalent to ‹spell/divination›, using the filidh's level as the caster level. The filidh and all allies who can hear his performance receive the information provided by the divination as flashes of inspiration and knowledge." special="Divinatory song takes 10 minutes and uses 6 rounds of bardic performance. Divinatory song relies on audible components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Voices of Life (Su)]{next l=8 icon=magic ability="A filidh can grant himself and all allies who can hear his performance the ability to speak with animals and plants as if affected by ‹spell/speak with animals› and ‹spell/speak with plants› so long as he maintains this performance." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Unity of Life (Su)]{next icon=magic-protect l=15 ability="A filidh can use his performance to interlink the life force of two allies who can hear his performance as though they were affected by ‹spell/shield other›, using the filidh's bard level as his caster level. The filidh designates which ally is the warded target and which ally receives half the warded creature's damage." order=af free="The filidh can switch the targets of this ability (and the effect of the performance the targets receive) once per round at the start of each turn that he maintains the performance." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Song of the Cycle (Su)]{next l=20 icon=protect ability="A filidh can grant awe-inspiring glimpses into the future with his magic. All allies who can see and hear the filidh are affected as though by the personal version of ‹spell/foresight› for the duration of the performance." replace="Deadly performance"}

first_world_minstrel.name: First World Minstrel
description: ## First World Minstrel
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/11›

Most cultures tell stories of people stolen away by the fey, only to return years later, forever changed. The First World minstrel was changed in such a way by the fey, and his performances channel the mysterious powers of the First World.
::ab[Fey Magic]{icon=learn note="A First World minstrel adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spells to his spell list instead of the equivalent ‹spell/summon monster› spells." alter="Spell list"}

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A First World minstrel gains the ‹ability/wild empathy› druid class feature, using his bard level as his druid level for this ability." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A First World minstrel gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Echoes of the First World (Su)]{next icon=power ability="A First World minstrel infuses himself or a single willing ally within 30 feet with the strange magic of the First World, granting the target one of the special abilities from the ‹template/fey creature« template› other than *change shape* for 1 round." special="For every 3 levels beyond 1st the First World minstrel has, he can target one additional ally or add an additional special ability to one of his targets. This is a polymorph effect that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Gremlin's Luck (Su)]{next icon=lower-roll ability="A First World minstrel can use his performance to inflict bad luck on a target within 30 feet for 1 round. Whenever the target rolls an ability check, attack roll, saving throw, or skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result. A target that succeeds at a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the First World minstrel's level + his Charisma modifier) negates the effect and is immune to this ability for 24 hours." special="This is a mind-affecting effect that relies on audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}
::ab[Resist Nature's Lure (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A First World minstrel gains the ‹ability/resist nature's lure› druid class feature." replace=Well-versed}

flame_dancer.name: Flame Dancer
description: ## Flame Dancer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/81›

A flame dancer studies the movements of fire, adding its grace to his repertoire. He seeks truth in fire's burning essence, and uses his performance to unleash the power of fire against those who dare oppose him.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A flame dancer gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Fire Dance (Su)]{next icon=def-protect l=1 flavor="A fire dancer learns to protect himself and others from the pain of fire, and discovers how to control the flame while enduring its heat." ability="Each round of the *fire dance,* he rolls a Perform (dance or sing) check. Any ally within 30 feet of the bard that has caught on fire or is affected by a fire effect or extreme heat can use the bard's Perform check result in place of its saving throw against that fire. Any ally within 30 feet of the bard who is suffering from ‹rule/heat«stroke› can ignore the fatigue from heat exposure so long as the bard maintains this performance." special="*Fire dance* relies on audible or visual components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Song of the Fiery Gaze (Su)]{next l=3 icon=power flavor="A fire dancer can allow allies to see through flames without any distortion. Any ally within 30 feet of the bard who can hear the performance can see through fire, fog, and smoke without penalty as long as the light is sufficient to allow him to see normally, as with the base effect of the ‹mystery/gaze of »flame«s› oracle revelation." special="*Song of the fiery gaze* relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Fire Break (Su)]{next l=6 icon=protect flavor="A fire dancer's performance can bend flames away from others." ability="Any ally within 30 feet of the bard who can hear or see the bardic performance gains ‹umr/resist› fire 20 as long as the performance is maintained." imp11="This resistance increases to 30." special="*Fire break* relies on audible or visual components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Fan the Flames (Su)]{icon=learn l=8 note="A fire dancer adds ‹spell/burning hands›, ‹spell/flaming sphere›, and ‹spell/fireball› to his list of bard spells known (as 1st-, 2nd-, and 3rd-level spells, respectively)." replace="Dirge of doom"}

flamesinger.name: Flamesinger
description: ## Flamesinger
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/5›

The flamesinger finds inspiration in the mesmerizing beauty of fire. While the flamesinger's tradition originated among the varied peoples of the Padishah Empire of Kelesh, it long ago spread to Qadira and from there throughout the Inner Sea region.

::ab[Fire Music]{icon=power-learn l1="A flamesinger gains ‹feat/Fire Music› as a bonus feat (without needing the meet the normal prerequisites)." l4="When she gains access to 2nd-level spells (4th level), she adds ‹spell/summon monster I› and ‹spell/summon monster II› to her bard spells known; every 3 levels thereafter (7th, 10th, 13th, 16th) she gains the next level of *summon monster,* until she adds ‹spell/summon monster VI› to her spells known at 16th level." special="The flamesinger must apply the Fire Music feat's effect when casting these spells." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A flamesinger gains the following bardic performance."}

::ab[Blazing Blades (Su)]{next icon=boost-magic ability="The flamesinger's song causes allies' weapons to burst into flames. To be affected, an ally must be able to hear the bard perform and be within 30 feet. An affected creature's successful attacks with manufactured or natural weapons deal 1d4 additional points of fire damage. These flames do not harm the weapon or its wielder. This damage stacks with other sources of fire damage, such as the ‹magic-enh/flaming› special weapon ability." imp5="The bonus damage becomes 2d4." imp11="The bonus damage becomes 3d4." imp17="The bonus damage becomes 4d4." special="This is a fire effect that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Wildfire (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="Like a raging wildfire, a flamesinger moves with blistering speed." l2="The flamesinger gains a +5-foot enhancement bonus to her base speed." l6="The bonus increases to +10 feet." l10="The bonus increases to +15 feet." l14="The bonus increases to +20 feet." l18="The bonus increases to +25 feet." replace="Versatile performance"}

fortune_teller.name: Fortune-Teller
description: ## Fortune-Teller
‹SOURCE Villain Codex/32›

Instead of using song and dance, a fortune-teller influences people by divining their fate. Though he learns a great deal about the paths of destiny, his true skill is in making others think he knows more than he truly does.

::ab[Fortune-Teller's Divinations]{icon=learn note="Add the following spells to the fortune-teller's bard spell list. He doesn't automatically gain them and must still select them as spells known if he wants to use them. 0-‹spell/guidance›; 1st-‹spell/object reading›; 2nd-‹spell/augury›; 3rd-‹spell/analyze aura›; 4th-‹spell/divination›; 5th-‹spell/retrocognition›; 6th-‹spell/true seeing›, ‹spell/vision›." alter="Spell list"}

:::ab{flavor="A fortune-teller can use Perform (oratory) to read a fortune as part of a bardic performance." icon=magic action=Ability title="Oracular Performance" alter="Bardic performance" replace=Countersong~distraction}

Once per hour when the bard performs a fortune-telling using a fortune-teller's deck, a crystal ball, or a similar item, he can read the fortune of one subject of the bardic performance. This is part of the same action used to activate the performance. The fortune-teller rolls d%, adds his bard level to the result, and consults the list below. The outcome depends on whether the chosen creature is an ally (for a beneficial performance) or an enemy (for a detrimental performance).
- Ally: **Woe**
- Enemy: **Weal**
- **Inconclusive**
- Ally: **Weal**
- Enemy: **Woe**

On an inconclusive result, the bard fails to get any information from the reading and the subject is affected by the performance normally. For an ally, on a weal result the subject's bonus from the performance increases by 1, and on a woe result the subject's bonus decreases by 1 (minimum 0). This affects only numeric bonuses. For example, a subject wouldn't gain another Hit Die from *inspire greatness.*

For an enemy, on a weal result the subject gains a +2 bonus on its saving throw to negate the bard's performance, and on a woe result it takes a -2 penalty on its save. An oracular performance has no effect on an enemy if the performance doesn't require a saving throw.

All adjustments from an oracular performance on a foe last only for that instance of the bardic performance. All adjustments on an ally last for 1 minute, applying each time the fortune-teller uses that particular bardic performance on that ally but ending when the duration expires, even if the performance continues.

::ab[Fortune-Teller's Acumen]{l=2 icon=magic ability="Whenever he casts a spell from the divination school, a fortune-teller can use a fortune-teller's deck, crystal ball, or similar item instead of the spell's material component, as long as the cost of the material component is no more than 100 gp per bard level. If he chooses to cast the spell using such an item and the spell's normal material component (regardless of that component's cost), he casts the spell at caster level." special="This special focus can't be used with other special foci that have a similar effect, but can be used with spells that normally require a focus component (which must also be provided)." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A fortune-teller gains the following bardic performance."}

:::ab{next title="Transparent Fate (Su)" l=8 icon=lower-aura action=Ability special="A creature that was targeted by oracular performance when this performance began applies any save DC adjustment to this secondary save as well. At the GM's discretion, the revelation of the creature's fate might have other consequences; for example, a creature's allies might see it was planning to betray them." replace="Dirge of doom"}

The fortune-teller can cause all enemies within 30 feet to have their near future revealed for all to see. A successful Will saving throw (DC = 10 ‹misc/half› the fortune-teller's level the fortune-teller's Cha modifier) negates the effect. This ability causes any creature attacked by an affected enemy to gain a +2 bonus to AC and on saving throws against the attack.

As a secondary effect, if an affected creature's hit points are reduced to less than its Constitution score, it also sees a vision of its own death and must succeed at another Will save or become frightened for 1 round. A given creature needs to save against this secondary effect at most once per performance of transparent fate, even if its hit points are reduced to less than its Constitution score multiple times.
